name: Patatas Bravas
tagline: Crisp-fried potatoes with smoky bravas sauce and garlic aioli
highlight: Twice-fried potatoes, smoky chili sauce, garlic aioli

hero.alt: Patatas bravas with bravas sauce and aioli
hero.caption: patatas bravas — smoky and crisp

healthFacts: Twice-frying creates a hard shell that holds heat without oil-logging.
Smoked paprika gives the bravas its signature smoke.

steps.title: Cube and parboil
steps.detail: Cube potatoes into 3/4-inch pieces. Parboil in salted water 6 min; drain and dry on a tray.
steps.tip: Drying time is non-negotiable — wet potatoes = soggy fries.

steps.title: Fry crispy
steps.detail: Heat oil to 350°F in a heavy pot. Fry potatoes in batches 6–7 min until deep gold and crisp. Drain on rack, salt while hot.
steps.tip: Restless potatoes = soggy. Let them sit and color.

steps.title: Whisk bravas
steps.detail: Warm tomato sauce with both paprikas, sherry vinegar, and a pinch of salt. Should pool, not run.
steps.tip: Bravas means 'fierce' — don't be shy with the heat.

steps.title: Aioli and plate
steps.detail: Mash garlic to a paste with salt. Stir into mayo with lemon. Plate potatoes, drizzle bravas, dot with aioli.
steps.tip: Real aioli is just garlic and oil emulsified, but mayo + raw garlic is a faithful shortcut.
